title: larix griffithii var. mastersiana
intro: Larix griffithii var. mastersiana, also known as Masters' larch, is a deciduous coniferous tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant's bark and leaves are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Larix griffithii var. mastersiana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being added to various formulations for its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
